text: I feel curious but a bit unsettled. The empty conference room, with its neatly arranged chairs and humming overhead lights, hints at anticipation—a gathering about to happen or one that just ended. I’m a silent observer in the threshold, feeling the weight of lingering expectations and fleeting conversations. Stepping into the next space, the bright colors and bold text pulse with ideas of research and development. This room seems to invite reflection, almost demanding I consider the fine line between intention and perception. I feel exposed here, as if my own uncertainties are made more visible by the thoughtful displays and words glowing on screens. There’s an undercurrent of vulnerability, but also the possibility of connection and growth. Each room asks me to consider not just what I see, but how I am seen, and how the nature of understanding itself is always in flux.
